rules.condition: [Patch rule — melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus] The lesion has a roughly oval shape with peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border; Darker pigment is concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than being uniformly distributed or centrally concentrated, creating an uneven pigment architecture; The lesion contains multiple shades of brown with areas that appear slightly lighter centrally compared to the mid-zone ring
rules.action: Classify as Melanoma. Confidence: medium. Rule: When a roughly oval brown lesion shows peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border AND darker pigment is concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than centrally, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: The lesion has a roughly oval shape with peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border
Darker pigment is concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than being uniformly distributed or centrally concentrated, creating an uneven pigment architecture
The lesion contains multiple shades of brown with areas that appear slightly lighter centrally compared to the mid-zone ring
rules.rule_text: When a roughly oval brown lesion shows peripheral dots/globules forming a corona-like pattern around the border AND darker pigment is concentrated in a mid-zone ring rather than centrally, classify as Melanoma.

rules.condition: [Patch rule — melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus] Numerous fine granular gray-brown dots and pepper-like specks scattered irregularly across the lesion, not forming any organized or repeating pattern; Multiple white-to-pink structureless patches (paler than surrounding skin) interspersed within the lesion, appearing as irregular pale islands among pigmented areas; At least 3 distinct color tones visible across the lesion — including tan/light brown, dark brown, gray, and white-pink — distributed in a disorganized, asymmetric arrangement
rules.action: Classify as Melanoma. Confidence: high. Rule: When a lesion shows scattered granular gray-brown dots and blotches distributed irregularly across the lesion, multiple white-pink structureless patches interspersed among pigmented areas, and an overall chaotic mix of at least 3 distinct color tones (tan, dark brown, gray, and white-pink), classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: Numerous fine granular gray-brown dots and pepper-like specks scattered irregularly across the lesion, not forming any organized or repeating pattern
Multiple white-to-pink structureless patches (paler than surrounding skin) interspersed within the lesion, appearing as irregular pale islands among pigmented areas
At least 3 distinct color tones visible across the lesion — including tan/light brown, dark brown, gray, and white-pink — distributed in a disorganized, asymmetric arrangement
rules.rule_text: When a lesion shows scattered granular gray-brown dots and blotches distributed irregularly across the lesion, multiple white-pink structureless patches interspersed among pigmented areas, and an overall chaotic mix of at least 3 distinct color tones (tan, dark brown, gray, and white-pink), classify as Melanoma.

rules.condition: [Patch rule — melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus] The lesion contains a central zone that is gray-blue or gray-white (lighter than the surrounding pigmented rim), appearing as a pale area within the darker border — indicating regression or loss of pigment in the center; The brown pigment at the lesion periphery is unevenly distributed, with variable shades of dark brown to tan forming an asymmetric ring or partial ring around the central pale area; The surrounding skin shows signs of chronic sun damage: scattered faint tan-yellow patches (solar lentigines) and fine pink-red linear vessels visible in the background
rules.action: Classify as Melanoma. Confidence: high. Rule: When a small dark lesion shows a central gray-blue or gray-white structureless zone surrounded by an irregular brown rim, and the lesion sits on sun-damaged skin with scattered background solar lentigines, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: The lesion contains a central zone that is gray-blue or gray-white (lighter than the surrounding pigmented rim), appearing as a pale area within the darker border — indicating regression or loss of pigment in the center
The brown pigment at the lesion periphery is unevenly distributed, with variable shades of dark brown to tan forming an asymmetric ring or partial ring around the central pale area
The surrounding skin shows signs of chronic sun damage: scattered faint tan-yellow patches (solar lentigines) and fine pink-red linear vessels visible in the background
rules.rule_text: When a small dark lesion shows a central gray-blue or gray-white structureless zone surrounded by an irregular brown rim, and the lesion sits on sun-damaged skin with scattered background solar lentigines, classify as Melanoma.
